valueprop_card_1_body: Run your PA program against the five-metric rubric: approval rate, turnaround time, appeal-overturn rate, administrative load, and member abandonment. Each scores green, yellow, or red.
valueprop_card_2_body: A high appeal-overturn rate means the PA was denying care it should have approved. A high abandonment rate means members gave up at the counter. Both are PA doing harm, not control.
valueprop_card_3_body: Some PA saves real money on genuinely misused drugs. Some is administrative theater that delays therapy and generates appeals. The scorecard tells them apart.
valueprop_card_4_body: Score the program, flag the red metrics, and bring the documented friction points to your PBM. Require turnaround-time and overturn-rate reporting in the next contract cycle.

Title: Rebate Report Audit Worksheet
headline: Your PBM Reports 100% Pass-Through. Audit the Noun.
headline_emphasis: The Audit Worksheet to Reconcile the Rebate Report.
subtitle: A 2-page printable worksheet PBS uses across 100s of PBM contract reviews annually. Free download. Sent to your inbox.
bullet_1: Three audit passes on the rebate report your PBM sends
bullet_2: Reconcile 'received' rebates against manufacturer-paid rebates
bullet_3: Where a rebate aggregator's cut hides before pass-through
bullet_4: Paste-ready broker request for the missing detail
valueprop_card_1_body: Open your most recent rebate report. Pull rebate-per-claim and total rebate by drug. The summary number is designed to reassure; the per-drug detail is where the reconciliation happens.
valueprop_card_2_body: '100% pass-through' usually means 100% of what the PBM received, not what the manufacturer paid. If a rebate aggregator took a cut first, the two numbers differ, and only one is on your report.
valueprop_card_3_body: Check how the contract defines 'rebate.' A narrow definition leaves market-share payments, admin allowances, and price-protection rebates outside the pass-through clause entirely.
valueprop_card_4_body: Run the three passes, document the gap between received and paid, and send the paste-ready request for aggregator-level detail to your broker before the next quarterly review.

valueprop_card_1_body: Five terms decide whether your GER guarantee means anything: the generic definition, the included-claims set, the reconciliation method, the exclusion list, and the true-up timing. The PBM controls four of them.
valueprop_card_2_body: Pull your generic effective rate and compare the guaranteed number to actual performance. A guarantee hit on paper while your plan overpays in practice is the GER game working as designed.
valueprop_card_3_body: GER guarantees are met by what they exclude. Single-source generics, specialty generics, and reclassified drugs carved out of the calculation are where the guaranteed average and your real cost diverge.
valueprop_card_4_body: Run the three passes, reconcile guaranteed against actual, and require the exclusion list and reconciliation method in writing. A GER guarantee you cannot audit is a number, not a guarantee.

valueprop_card_1_body: Five terms set your network: the preferred-pharmacy definition, the exclusion criteria, the mail-order mandate, the specialty carve-out, and the out-of-network penalty. Together they decide where members are allowed to fill.
valueprop_card_2_body: Pull where claims actually fill and compare it to the network the contract describes. A routing pattern concentrated in PBM-owned pharmacies beyond what the design requires is a steering finding.

valueprop_card_4_body: Run the three passes, document the routing-versus-design gap, and require the exclusion rationale in writing. A network you did not design is a network designed for someone else's margin.

valueprop_card_1_body: Five clauses protect members through a transition: continuity-of-care, prior-auth honoring, refill-too-soon override, formulary grandfathering, and member-communication timing. Plan sponsors assume they execute automatically.

valueprop_card_1_body: Five terms decide your exit leverage: the notice window, the termination-for-cause triggers, the claims-data ownership, the runout-claim pricing, and the transition-assistance obligation. Most plans never read them until they want out.
valueprop_card_2_body: Auto-renewal plus a long notice window plus PBM-owned claims data equals a plan locked in without knowing it. The audit surfaces the lock-in while you still have time to renegotiate it.

valueprop_card_4_body: Run the three passes, document the lock-in terms, and renegotiate data ownership and runout pricing at renewal. Leverage you can only use at exit has to be written in long before you exit.

Title: Definition Variance Audit
headline: Same Words. Different Meanings.
headline_emphasis: Across Renewal Cycles, the Definitions Quietly Shrink.
subtitle: A 2-page printable worksheet PBS uses across 100s of PBM contract reviews annually. Free download. Sent to your inbox.
bullet_1: Three audit passes across your contract's definitions

bullet_3: Where 'generic,' 'brand,' and 'specialty' quietly drifted
bullet_4: The silent drift that shrinks every guarantee
valueprop_card_1_body: The first pass gathers your current and prior contract versions. Definition drift is invisible in any single contract; it only shows up when you line up 'generic,' 'brand,' 'specialty,' and 'rebate' across versions.
valueprop_card_2_body: Across renewal cycles the definitions quietly shrink. A drug reclassified from 'generic' to 'specialty' moves it into the bucket with the weakest guarantee, with no change to the headline discount.
valueprop_card_3_body: Every guarantee is only as strong as the definition under it. The third pass connects each drifted definition to the guarantee it weakens, so the cost of a wording change becomes visible.
valueprop_card_4_body: Run the three passes, document every definition that drifted, and lock the definitions at renewal. The most expensive edits to a PBM contract are the ones to the words, not the numbers.
